description: Production-ready Helm chart for deploying Steampipe on Kubernetes. Steampipe is the zero-ETL way to query cloud APIs with SQL. This chart deploys Steampipe in service mode as a persistent PostgreSQL endpoint with plugin management, optional OAuth2 Proxy authentication, and support for 140+ cloud provider plugins.
rules: Steampipe runs as UID 9193, GID 0 (OpenShift compatible). Always set podSecurityContext.fsGroup=9193, runAsUser=9193, runAsGroup=0
Use bbdd.enabled=true to expose the PostgreSQL endpoint as a Kubernetes Service on port 9193
Set STEAMPIPE_DATABASE_PASSWORD via a Kubernetes Secret for stable database passwords across restarts
Plugin .spc configuration files must be mounted into /home/steampipe/.steampipe/config/ using extraVolumes and extraVolumeMount
The initContainer installs plugins declared in initContainer.plugins[] before the main container starts
Steampipe v2.x removed dashboard and mods support — use Powerpipe (https://powerpipe.io) for dashboards and compliance benchmarks
The Ingress template exposes an HTTP/L7 endpoint — Steampipe uses PostgreSQL/TCP on port 9193, so a TCP-capable Ingress controller is required (e.g., NGINX TCP passthrough)
Set STEAMPIPE_UPDATE_CHECK=false and STEAMPIPE_TELEMETRY=none for Kubernetes deployments
Always use --foreground flag (set in args by default) for container deployments
